channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Musk’s crew descends on CFPB with plan to shut it down
item.description: The CFPB is funded independently through the Federal Reserve, but the CFPB director has the power to determine how much money the agency needs. That means a CFPB director could zero out the agency's budget entirely.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg News, The Mercury News

item.title: These groups are pushing for the NFL to end facial recognition
item.description: Ahead of Super Bowl Sunday, online privacy groups Fight for the Future and the Algorithmic Justice League are reiterating a call for the NFL to put an end to the use of facial recognition in football stadiums, where the groups say the technology is used to authenticate employees, vendors, and author
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Yandex Releases Open-Source Tool to Identify and Optimize Code Inefficiencies - The Impact
item.description: Yandex releases Perforator, an open-source tool that identifies code inefficiencies and offers suggestions for optimization, potentially reducing infrastructure costs by up to 20%.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Academic researchers are going to have to learn algospeak
item.description: Imagine you’re an academic researcher. You’re writing a pitch for funding to the National Science Foundation (NSF), the independent agency of the federal government that funds projects designed to advance our understanding of the world.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How AI could replace government workers to reduce spending
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE are reportedly using artificial intelligence to analyze federal spending and where cuts an be made
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Apple could soon be forced to give away all your encrypted data to the UK government
item.description: The UK issued the order under the controversial Investigatory Powers Act. Now, experts fear Meta and Google could be the next target, warning against mass surveillance.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: DeepSeek AI app should be banned from US government devices, lawmakers say
item.description: US lawmakers want the DeepSeek app banned from government devices, saying it poses a threat to US national security.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Google Messages could soon let you delete messages for other people, and I can't wait to pretend my cringiest jokes never happened
item.description: Google has added a new unsend feature to the most recent unreleased version of Google Messages, which will allow you to delete messages for everybody in a chat rather than only yourself.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Clínica Universidad de Navarra apuesta por la IA y la computación cuántica en el ámbito sanitario
item.description: La Clínica Universidad de Navarra (CUN) y Microsoft Ibérica han firmado un acuerdo de colaboración para promover el desarrollo conjunto de proyectos innovadores en el sector salud, aplicando para ello tecnologías avanzadas como la inteligencia artificial (IA) o la computación cuántica.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: LG recalls 500K electric ovens after fires, pet deaths
item.description: According to the recall, the front-mounted knobs on the recalled ranges can be accidentally activated by humans or pets.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, NewsNation

item.title: Bridgestone mejora las capacidades de desarrollo virtual de neumáticos con su propio simulador
item.description: Bridgestone, la compañía especializada en neumáticos y soluciones de movilidad sostenible, ha avanzado la inversión de un nuevo simulador de conducción en circuito (DiL, por sus siglas en inglés) en su centro de I+D en la región de EMEA.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The number of venomous snakes removed from this Australian yard will make you shudder
item.description: A man described feeling “the shudders” as more than 100 venomous red-bellied black snakes were removed from a pile of mulch in his Sydney backyard.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hacked on social? Here’s what to do next
item.description: Spambots, data thieves, and other bad actors run rampant online — more so now than ever before. Here's what to do if one gets ahold of you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Quantum networking advances on Earth and in space
item.description: Cisco’s Quantum Summit brought together quantum computing experts last week from around academia, government, and industry and highlighted the progress we’ve seen over the past few months.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Europe’s DMA gives another big boost to iOS platform decay
item.description: One of a handful of “independent” iOS app stores in Europe has begun the distribution of a porn app, advertising it as “Apple Approved.” The app isn’t approved by Apple, and the biggest app available on the AltStore is Epic’s child-focused Fortnite game.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Linux containers in 2025 and beyond
item.description: The use of Linux containers has shown no sign of slowing down since their emergence in the early 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to easily transfer files and photos off a computer to your hard drive
item.description: It's not just about freeing up space on your computer, Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says. It's about protecting your digital memories and important documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CISA hires former DHS CIO into top cyber position
item.description: Karen Evans is returning to the Department of Homeland Security in a top cyber job as DHS responds to major hacks into U.S. critical infrastructure. The post CISA hires former DHS CIO into top cyber position first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
